purpose: Canonical durable persistence, entitlement counter, ownership resolution, and transactional delivery-grant foundation for the MMG Knowledge Subscription ecosystem.

database_contract.transaction_requirement: Every picker mutation and every confirmation grant set is persisted inside one database transaction.

ownership_contract.owned_definition: A customer owns an asset when at least one active non-revoked grant exists for the canonical asset_id.
ownership_contract.deduplication_rule: The customer-facing library displays one entry per asset_id even if audit history contains multiple historical grants.
ownership_contract.selection_rule: An actively owned asset is excluded from subscription selection and consumes no new entitlement units.
ownership_contract.active_uniqueness: At most one active ownership grant may exist per customer_id and asset_id.

entitlement_counter_contract.formula: remaining units = total units - selected units - reserved units - confirmed units
entitlement_counter_contract.package_counter: remaining packages = total packages - confirmed packages

required_checks: The entitlement window row is locked and its version equals expectedPreviousVersion.
The exact target title count is present.
The exact window unit total is consumed.
Every selected asset remains an active published available digital download.
Every selected asset remains subscription eligible.
Every selected asset has verified portrait and square media.
Every selected asset has a verified delivery package reference.
No selected asset is already actively owned by the customer.
The cycle retains sufficient package and unit capacity.
atomic_writes: Create one delivery grant per selected asset.
Create one active ownership grant per selected asset.
Increment cycle confirmed-package and consumed-unit counters.
confirmation_transaction_contract.rollback_rule: Any failed eligibility, ownership, capacity, grant, or version check rolls back the entire confirmation transaction.

picker_repository_contract.load_behavior: Resolve the authenticated customer's active subscription, current entitlement window, selections, recent idempotency keys, active ownership grants, and current catalog projection.
picker_repository_contract.save_behavior: Use row locking and expected-version comparison before synchronizing selections and request IDs.

security_contract: Derive customer identity exclusively from the authenticated server session.
Never accept customer, subscription, cycle, or window identity from public Liquid or browser calculations.
Use parameterized SQL for all customer and asset values.
Use database row locks and expected versions for concurrent writes.
Keep entitlement responses private and non-cacheable.
Preserve audit history for grants, requests, and entitlement events.
Never represent client-side counters as authoritative.

integration_sequence.current_component: MMG Entitlement Counter and Ownership-Resolution Persistence

release_gates: The PostgreSQL migration has been reviewed, backed up, and applied in the production data environment.
A production SQL pool is connected to MMGPostgresDatabase through server-only environment configuration.
The deployed authenticated API adapter maps the session to the canonical customer ID.
Shopify subscription contracts are reconciled into mmg_subscription_entitlements.
Billing cycles and package windows are created from the locked plan contract.
The asset projection is synchronized from verified Shopify and Kairos metadata.
At least two subscription-selectable assets have verified delivery packages.
Concurrent selection and confirmation tests pass against a real PostgreSQL transaction environment.
The entitlement counter is integrated without replacing the existing Public Catalog experience.
Backup, rollback, observability, and grant-repair procedures are approved before live customer use.
